chief_complaint: A 35-year-old male with type 1 diabetes (T1D) presents with symptoms of hyperglycemia and ketosis.
history: The patient has a history of poorly controlled T1D, with frequent episodes of hyperglycemia and ketosis. He relies on rapid-acting insulin analogs (RAA) and ultra-rapid-acting insulin analogs (URAA) for postprandial glycemic control. He has also experienced episodes of hypoglycemia and has concerns about insulin stacking. He is physically active and engages in regular exercise.
exam: Vital signs: blood pressure 120/80 mmHg, pulse 100 beats per minute, respiratory rate 20 breaths per minute. Physical examination reveals no abnormalities.
labs: Blood glucose: 350 mg/dL, ketones: 3+, ECG: normal.
